abstract: This entry refers to a 2025 piece titled 'The State of Social Media Research APIs & Tools in the Digital Service Act Era.' No abstract or full text was available for review, so substantive content cannot be summarized. The title indicates a focus on the landscape of social media research infrastructure following the implementation of the EU's Digital Services Act (DSA).
key_claims: No claims can be extracted; the source text was not available.
findings: Not available; no abstract or body text was provided.
contributions: Cannot be determined from the title alone, though the work appears positioned to survey or assess the current ecosystem of social media research APIs and tools under the DSA regulatory regime.
framing: Based on the title, the work situates itself within ongoing debates about platform research access, data governance, and the regulatory implications of the EU Digital Services Act for computational social science and platform studies.
